rationale: Educ. Code §11.178 prohibits school districts from using resources for hotels. It is unrelated to the residence homestead exemption amount in Tax Code §11.13(b). No conflict exists.
quote: The board of trustees of an independent school district may not impose taxes, issue bonds, use or authorize the use of school district employees, use or authorize the use of school district property, money, or other resources, or acquire property for the design, construction, renovation, or operation of a hotel.
_note: Rule text concerns hotel prohibitions; no overlap with homestead exemption valuation.
